prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Beginning in early November 2022, demonstrations commenced at multiple sites across Ireland. These protests emerged following the establishment of temporary refugee shelters in various regions of the nation by the Department of Children, Equality, Disability, Integration and Youth (DCEDIY), in its effort to house the 65,000 refugees who arrived throughout 2022. These demonstrations have taken place in areas such as East Wall, Ballymun, Drimnagh, and other parts of Dublin, as well as in Fermoy and Mallow in County Cork, Kill in County Kildare, Lismore in County Waterford, and Mullingar. The concerns voiced during these protests consistently included perceived discrimination in refugee selection and the failure to provide advance notice to local residents. Furthermore, demonstrators have challenged the absence of women and children in certain accommodation facilities. The involvement of anti-immigrant activists, individuals affiliated with far-right organizations, and violent offenders at these protests has been prominently noted online and through various other channels.
B. Following the development of temporary refugee shelters by DCEDIY in different parts of Ireland, protests began in early November 2022. Protests were held in East Wall, Ballymun, Drimnagh, and elsewhere in Dublin, Fermoy and Mallow in County Cork, Kill, County Kildare, Lismore, County Waterford, and Mullingar. Concerns were raised about refugee selection discrimination and the lack of women and children in some accommodation centres.
C. Protests at several locations in Ireland started in early November 2022 after the development of sites in various parts of the country as temporary refugee shelters by the Department of Children, Equality, Disability, Integration and Youth (DCEDIY), as it attempts to accommodate the influx of 65,000 refugees during 2022. Protests have been held in East Wall, Ballymun, Drimnagh and elsewhere in Dublin, Fermoy and Mallow in County Cork, Kill, County Kildare, Lismore, County Waterford, and Mullingar. The protests raised similar concerns over refugee selection discrimination and the lack of provision of prior information to residents. Protesters have questioned the lack of women and children in some accommodation centres.The presence of anti-immigrant protesters, members of far-right groups, and violent criminals at these protests has been highlighted online and in

Answer:

options.A: Beginning in early November 2022, demonstrations commenced at multiple sites across Ireland. These protests emerged following the establishment of temporary refugee shelters in various regions of the nation by the Department of Children, Equality, Disability, Integration and Youth (DCEDIY), in its effort to house the 65,000 refugees who arrived throughout 2022. These demonstrations have taken place in areas such as East Wall, Ballymun, Drimnagh, and other parts of Dublin, as well as in Fermoy and Mallow in County Cork, Kill in County Kildare, Lismore in County Waterford, and Mullingar. The concerns voiced during these protests consistently included perceived discrimination in refugee selection and the failure to provide advance notice to local residents. Furthermore, demonstrators have challenged the absence of women and children in certain accommodation facilities. The involvement of anti-immigrant activists, individuals affiliated with far-right organizations, and violent offenders at these protests has been prominently noted online and through various other channels.
options.B: Following the development of temporary refugee shelters by DCEDIY in different parts of Ireland, protests began in early November 2022. Protests were held in East Wall, Ballymun, Drimnagh, and elsewhere in Dublin, Fermoy and Mallow in County Cork, Kill, County Kildare, Lismore, County Waterford, and Mullingar. Concerns were raised about refugee selection discrimination and the lack of women and children in some accommodation centres.
options.C: Protests at several locations in Ireland started in early November 2022 after the development of sites in various parts of the country as temporary refugee shelters by the Department of Children, Equality, Disability, Integration and Youth (DCEDIY), as it attempts to accommodate the influx of 65,000 refugees during 2022.
